free_text: Differential neutron emission cross sections from lead
and carbon bombarded with 14 MeV neutrons.
Operated in pulse regime with deuteron
pulses of 2 nsec F.W.H.M and 5 MHz repetition.
Deuteron beam of 30 microA and Ti-T target
Flight path of 4.9 m perpendicularly to the

in diameter and 3.8 cm length coupled with a XP 2041
-TOF spectrometry of the neutrons emitted from Cf-252

and was also calculated using a Monte-Carlo code.
The source strength was determined by counting the
a-particles with a silicon surface-barrier detector.
